patterns.decisionQuestion: What evidence, root cause, impact, and history justify resolving, escalating, or sending back this record?
patterns.emptyStateCopy: No case is loaded. Return to the parent queue and reopen a row with evidence.

typography: Detail H1 uses text-2xl or smaller; card/rail section titles usually stay text-sm and only use text-base for a dominant section.
Evidence, history, and metadata rows use text-sm plus text-xs supporting copy.
density: At a desktop review viewport, first viewport shows identity/profile context, evidence or activity, and the primary resolution/action surface.
Detail must not become a stack of large cards with only one decision item visible.
spacing: Use gap-4/gap-5 for split panes; avoid default gap-6/space-y-6.
Cards inside detail panes use compact headers and padding=sm when possible.
borders: Use subtle outline-fg-grey-200 surfaces and light dividers for evidence/history rows.
profileRegion: Detail pages need a dedicated identity/profile rail or identity strip, not only a breadcrumb title.
Profile rail should carry status, owner, SLA/risk, and stable metadata.
Use responsive rail constraints; do not hard-code fixed pixel rails.
rightRail: Right/context rail uses responsive min/max or clamp constraints, stays compact, and remains subordinate to the main evidence/action surface.
Right rail contains activity/history/related records; it must not replace root cause, impact, or resolution.
Do not replace split-pane topology with a loose grid of unrelated cards.

forbiddenShortcuts: Do not render detail as a flat field list.
Do not hide resolution/escalation below secondary evidence.
Do not omit evidence, root cause, impact, history, audit trail, or next workflow link.
Do not use dashboard KPI rows as a replacement for evidence and decision context.
Do not pass visual acceptance with quality-eval text only; browser screenshot must show evidence/action surfaces.
